title: Icacorea jamaicensis
intro: Icacorea jamaicensis, also known as Jamaican dogwood, is a plant species native to the Caribbean region, traditionally used as a medicinal herb. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as pain, fever, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Jamaican dogwood include lignans, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Jamaican dogwood is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including pain, rheumatism, and fever.
taxonomy: Icacorea jamaicensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Icacinaceae. It is a member of the order Icacales and the clade Asterids. The plant is native to Jamaica, which is reflected in its specific epithet 'jamaicensis'. At the kingdom level, Icacorea jamaicensis is a Plantae, which includes all plant species. Further classification includes the division Magnoliophyta and the class Magnoliopsida.
